detail:
commerce.price

Generates a price between min and max (inclusive).

Schema: awd.domain.commerce.price()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
dec	The number of decimal places. Example: 2
max	The maximum price. Example: 100
min	The minimum price. Example: 1
symbol	The currency value to use. Example: $
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
dec	integer	optional
max	number	optional
min	number	optional
symbol	string	optional
Usage Examples

Shows commerce.price in use.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (dec=2, max=10, min=1, symbol="$")

Full call: commerce.price(dec=2, max=10, min=1, symbol="$")

Returns: $4.79

Shows commerce.price when optional params are omitted.

Command: commerce.price

Params fiel

detail:
helpers.arrayElement

Returns one random element from the supplied array.

Schema: faker.helpers.arrayElement()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
array	Array of candidate values to choose from.
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
array	array	required
Usage Examples

Shows helpers.arrayElement in use.

Command: helpers.arrayElement

Params field: (["A", "B", "C"])

Full call: helpers.arrayElement(["A", "B", "C"])

Returns: B

case: core enum via picker with values
detail:
enum

Enum values allow you to specify a list of discrete options. Use comma-separated values with no quotes, for example "active,inactive,pending" or "enum active,inactive,pending".

Schema: Enum()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
values	List of allowed values randomly selected during generation. Example: active,inactive,pending
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
values	comma-separated list	required
Usage Examples

Enum example usage.

Command: enum

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: enum active,inactive,pending

Open documentation
